news.headline: Team whispers: Blues hopeful over defender, Hawks star could return, Dee on track
news.body: Stay up to date with all the latest teams news ahead of round 12.
news.ai_summary: Hawthorn midfielder Will Day is in line to return for Round 12 after playing in the VFL last week — a key fantasy hold given his midfield role and scoring ceiling, so owners should monitor confirmation at team selection. Carlton is hopeful of regaining a defender, offering potential DPP/defensive relevance for coaches needing a value play. Melbourne also report a key Demon is on track to return, which could impact role and ownership decisions. Watch late mail closely before locking in trades and captains.

news.headline: Dual flag coach, new Swans mentor to lead Australia and Ireland rep footy sides
news.body: A dual premiership coach and a former captain will coach Australia, while Sydney's new coach will helm Ireland's side.
news.ai_summary: No fantasy relevance: this is an AFLW representative coaching announcement, with North Melbourne's Darren Crocker to coach Australia (assisted by Daisy Pearce) and Sydney's Colin O'Riordan to coach Ireland (assisted by Craig Starcevich) in the August 1 fixture at North Sydney Oval. No player selection, injury, role or price implications for SuperCoach or AFL Fantasy coaches.

news.headline: Hinkley calls on Bombers to make Hird call as Sheedy backs club great
news.body: Ken Hinkley believes the Bombers must first make a decision on whether they want James Hird as coach.

news.headline: 'There are three of us': Oliver keen to share focus with fellow ex-Dees
news.body: Clayton Oliver will face his former team Melbourne on Sunday in Alice Springs and he's trying to treat it as 'just another game'.

news.headline: The newest Magpie worked next door to his teammates, but they didn't know it
news.body: Mitch Podhajski went from podiatrist to Magpie when Collingwood swooped on him in the Telstra AFL Mid-Season Rookie Draft.
news.ai_summary: Collingwood used pick 17 in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft on 27-year-old VFL goalkicking leader Mitch Podhajski (27 goals in 8 games for Coburg), a 191cm mobile forward. As a mature-age rookie buried deep on the Pies' list behind an established forward line, he's not a fantasy consideration at minimum price and unlikely to debut soon. Monitor only if injuries open up a Round 13+ opportunity — the Podsiadly comparisons are flattering but speculative. Avoid.

news.headline: Giants skipper has played through nasty injury during injury crisis
news.body: Toby Greene has continued to play through injury since the Sydney Derby.

news.headline: Saints spearhead embraces full name, rich Maori heritage
news.body: Jesse Tawhiao-Wardlaw has proudly chosen to use her birth name as her cultural journey continues.
news.ai_summary: Note for fantasy coaches: St Kilda forward Jesse Wardlaw will now appear in team lists as Jesse Tawhiao-Wardlaw, so check your league platforms to ensure she's correctly identified at selection time. There are no injury, role or price changes attached to the news — she remains the Saints' key forward coming off a 75-game milestone season. Her three-goal final-quarter burst referenced in the article highlights her ceiling as a forward-line scoring option in AFLW Fantasy. Purely an admin/name-change watch, not a job-security or role shift.

news.headline: No bitterness, but Gowans concedes time was right to leave Swans
news.body: Scott Gowans insists his parting ways with Sydney was amicable, and that he didn't realise it was a blessing in disguise.
news.ai_summary: This is an AFLW coaching story with no relevance to SuperCoach or AFL Fantasy (which are men's competitions only). Former Sydney AFLW coach Scott Gowans has departed the club amicably and taken on a development role with the Eastern Ranges U18 Girls program. There are no selection, injury, role or price implications for fantasy coaches here.

news.headline: The 25: FIVE Dockers make our list of the best 25 players in the AFL
news.body: Welcome back to 'The 25', where AFL.com.au's team of experts rank the best players in the game.
news.ai_summary:
**Fantasy Implications from The 25:**

Tom Bolton is in career-best form (24.4 disposals, 4.4 clearances, 13 goals) as a midfield/forward and remains a strong POD option. Josh Treacy's career-high averages (13.8 disposals, 7.2 marks, 2.3 goals) make him a premium forward hold. Jy Simpkin... err, Jai Newcombe is producing career-best numbers (25.5 disposals, 7.6 clearances, 4.1 tackles) and is a must-have mid. Brodie Grundy (35.8 hitouts) is the clear #1 ruck, while Matt Rowell owners should monitor closely given injury-affected form, and Lachie Neale (30.7 disposals) is back to elite output.

news.headline: Why James Hird says he's the right man to make Essendon great again
news.body: Former Essendon coach James Hird says he would love to return to the club and succeed Brad Scott in the top job.

news.headline: BARRETT: Why re-appointing James Hird would be the most Essendon act, ever
news.body: Will the Bombers go back to their 'red and black security blanket' in the form of James Hird? It would be the most Essendon thing ever, writes Damian Barrett.
news.ai_summary: Minimal direct fantasy relevance, but coaching upheaval at Essendon matters for role/job security of Bombers assets. Brad Scott has been sacked with Dean Solomon installed as interim coach, meaning game plan, rotations and midfield/forward roles could shift in the back half of the season — monitor Merrett, Durham, Caddy and Wright for any usage changes. A potential James Hird appointment or new senior coach in 2026 could reshape list strategy and pre-season pricing, so keep Bombers premiums on watch rather than locking in long-term holds. No immediate injury or selection news for fantasy sides this week.

news.headline: Bombers land Tassie speedster at No.1, rucks galore, Pies target experience
news.body: Essendon has taken Jaxon Artemis with the No.1 pick in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft.
news.ai_summary: Essendon snapped up rebounding half-back Jaxon Artemis with pick No.1 in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft, and with 14 Bombers (including Sam Durham) ruled out for the West Coast trip, he's a genuine chance to debut immediately under caretaker Dean Solomon — making him a cheap rookie POD worth monitoring. The 19-year-old WA speedster offers damaging ball-use and run from defence, profiling similarly to Tom McCarthy's quick AFL impact at West Coast last year. Other fantasy-relevant selections include Richmond's Kye Annand, West Coast's Oliver Francou and Carlton's Flynn Riley, all potential cash-cow options if they earn senior call-ups. Wait for confirmed Round 11 teams before locking in Artemis.

news.headline: Why coaching contenders will be wary of vacant Essendon job
news.body: The litany of sacked coaches at Essendon in the past 20 years makes for grim reading if you're a young prospective senior coach, writes Cal Twomey.

news.headline: Demons forward retires after tough run with injuries
news.body: Demons forward Shane McAdam has retired.
news.ai_summary: Shane McAdam has called it quits after persistent injuries derailed his career. No fantasy relevance — leave him off your watchlist.

news.headline: Weightman on track for shock return, ruck still in protocols
news.body: Cody Weightman could come straight back into the AFL side after 20 months on the sidelines.
news.ai_summary: Weightman is in line for a shock senior return after 20 months out, putting him back on the fantasy radar as a cheap forward option. Meanwhile, the Dogs' ruck remains stuck in concussion protocols, leaving a selection cloud worth monitoring.

news.headline: Dogs hold off Demons in thriller for vital win
news.body: The Dogs have held off the Demons in a thriller.
news.ai_summary: The Bulldogs edged Melbourne in a nail-biter to bank a crucial win. Check the box scores for fantasy-relevant output from key Dogs and Dees.

news.headline: Nine things we learned: Leave this young Dockers star loose at your peril
news.body: The Giants' season is still alive, Richmond has found a bargain-basement recruit plus more lessons from round 11.
news.ai_summary: Don't sleep on Fremantle's emerging young gun—he's burning opponents when left unattended and shaping as a must-watch fantasy option. Round 11 also flagged GWS keeping their finals hopes alive and Richmond unearthing a cut-price recruit worth a look.

news.headline: Giant killers! Historic blitz sees GWS demolish reigning premiers
news.body: A 14-goal third-quarter has set Greater Western Sydney up for a shellacking of Brisbane.
news.ai_summary: GWS unleashed a record-breaking 14-goal third quarter to humiliate reigning premiers Brisbane. Expect huge fantasy scores from Giants midfielders and forwards, while Lions owners brace for ugly numbers across the board.

news.headline: Dogs, Dees lock in final teams for important clash. Follow LIVE
news.body: Follow all the action from Sunday's round 11 games.
news.ai_summary: Bulldogs and Demons have confirmed final teams for Sunday's crucial Round 11 clash — check your lockouts and finalise trades now. Live coverage rolling across all Sunday games.

news.headline: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dogs down Dees in classic
news.body: Greater Western Sydney has left reigning premier Brisbane reeling with a record-breaking goal surge, while the Bulldogs get home in a see-sawing contest against the Demons.
news.ai_summary: GWS unleashed a historic goal blitz to smash reigning premier Brisbane — Lions owners should brace for ugly scores, while Giants attackers are must-watch. The Bulldogs edged Melbourne in a thriller, keeping their high-scoring mids firmly in fantasy calculations.

news.headline: Dockers roll on with stars set to return, Lyon on 'bizarre' Wilkie treatment
news.body: Justin Longmuir's side is well placed to tackle the reigning premier at the Gabba in round 12.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle are humming and could welcome key stars back for a tough Gabba clash with Brisbane in Round 12 — monitor returning Dockers for potential POD value. Meanwhile, Lyon's "bizarre" handling of Wilkie raises a selection cloud worth watching before lockout.

news.headline: Ryan Angwin — cleared / returned to play
news.body: Ryan Angwin no longer appearing on injury/selection watch. Assumed fit.

news.headline: Simpkin's powerful No.36 tribute inspires stirring performance
news.body: After a tumultuous off-season also marred by tragedy, Jy Simpkin is adding to his lofty reputation this year.
news.ai_summary: Simpkin is in career-best form, channelling emotion into elite output and shaping as a must-consider midfield option. Coaches chasing a value-priced Roo gun should have him firmly on the radar.

news.headline: How Giants are driving cultural progress with new First Nations leaders
news.body: GWS has made two significant off-field appointments to help battle the decline in First Nations talent.
news.ai_summary: GWS has appointed two new First Nations leaders off-field to address the league-wide decline in Indigenous talent. No direct fantasy impact, but signals the Giants' long-term investment in player development pathways.

news.headline: The Lions are limping, but this is why you shouldn't write them off
news.body: The back-to-back premiers aren't in flag form but their best could be just around the corner.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane are scratchy but far from done — keep faith with your Lions premiums, as a form spike looks imminent.

news.headline: 'It's fun, winning': Fraser says Blues finding belief, Carr ready to 'reset' flickering Power
news.body: Successive wins has Carlton feeling good about itself, while Yartapuulti heads to its bye in need of a break.
news.ai_summary: Carlton's two-game winning streak is restoring confidence and could spark a fantasy revival for underperforming Blues; meanwhile, Port Adelaide limps into its bye, making their players risky holds until form returns.

news.headline: Why Pies' record-breaker isn't done yet, and the 'crazy' moment that made him uncomfortable
news.body: After breaking the all-time games record, Pies champion Scott Pendlebury isn't ready to stop yet.
news.ai_summary: Scott Pendlebury has no plans to retire after breaking the all-time AFL games record, signalling the Pies champ remains a viable midfield-forward option for fantasy coaches chasing durable, cash-cow-proof veterans.

news.headline: New Blues go back-to-back with big win over Power
news.body: Josh Fraser has extended his early perfect record in charge at Carlton with a strong win over Yartapuulti.
news.ai_summary: Carlton are 2-0 under new coach Josh Fraser after dispatching Port Adelaide, with the Blues' midfield rolling and looking like a fertile hunting ground for fantasy points.

news.headline: Cats sharpen claws to end Swans' winning streak
news.body: Geelong puts in claims as a serious flag contender after comfortably downing Sydney.
news.ai_summary: Geelong's emphatic win over Sydney stamps them as genuine flag threats, boosting the fantasy appeal of Cats premiums. Swans owners should monitor output closely after the streak-snapping loss exposed some cracks.

news.headline: Roos end long wait: Your club's last win with a goal after the siren
news.body: Check out the last time your club won, and lost, with a goal after the siren.
news.ai_summary: North Melbourne have finally broken their drought, joining the list of clubs to seal a win with a goal after the siren. Worth a look at how your club stacks up on the all-time list of last-gasp winners and losers.

news.headline: Roos go close to club record in Suns stunner: Your club's biggest comeback win
news.body: Check out your club's biggest ever comeback win.
news.ai_summary: North Melbourne nearly broke their club record for biggest comeback in a stunning win over the Suns — check where your club's greatest comeback ranks.

news.headline: Roo-diculous! Zurhaar sinks Suns after siren to secure epic comeback win
news.body: Cam Zurhaar nails the shot after the siren to give North Melbourne a six-point win.
news.ai_summary: Zurhaar's after-the-siren goal seals a stunning Roos comeback, boosting his fantasy relevance as a forward option in form. Gold Coast owners should be wary of late-game collapses denting their players' scoring upside.

news.headline: Bombers learn severity of injuries after brutal Dreamtime outing
news.body: The Bombers have confirmed the severity of several of the injuries they suffered on Friday night.
news.ai_summary: Trade out alert: Andrew McGrath (fractured jaw) and Matt Guelfi (hamstring tear) are both out until after Essendon's Round 15 bye, making them immediate trade targets before price drops hit. Sam Durham is in concussion protocols and will miss at least Round 12 vs West Coast — hold if you can stomach one week, but monitor closely. Jye Caldwell is under an injury cloud with an ankle/syndesmosis issue, adding further risk to Bomber owners. Archie May has been cleared and should play, offering some rare good news for cheapie holders.

news.headline: Brent Harvey is about to lose his place in AFL history
news.body: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.
news.ai_summary: Scott Pendlebury overtakes Brent Harvey today as the VFL/AFL games record holder, a largely ceremonial milestone with minimal fantasy impact given Pendlebury's well-established role and pricing. Harvey tips North Melbourne's Harry Sheezel as a future contender, citing his durability (just one game missed since being drafted in 2022) — a strong tick for fantasy coaches considering the premium mid as a long-term hold. No injury or role changes flagged for either player.

news.headline: The five-hour drive that started Sun's long road to an AFL spot
news.body: Oscar Adams is establishing himself as a regular for Gold Coast's best 23 but it wouldn't have happened without his mum's long hours behind the wheel.
news.ai_summary: Oscar Adams has cemented himself in Gold Coast's best 23, playing seven games in 2026 as a key defender alongside Sam Collins and Mac Andrew, taking the spot vacated by the dropped Charlie Ballard. His role as an intercepting "come-forward defender" is locked in for now, with Ballard out of favour reducing job-security risk. Coming off arguably his best game against Port in Darwin, he's a deep-league DPP defender option for keeper leagues rather than a short-term cash cow, given his low base price and improving output. Monitor his scoring trend before pulling the trigger in Fantasy/SuperCoach.

news.headline: ‘Bit of a floorshow’: Saints coach responds to ‘bizarre’ antics as AFL agitator causes stir - Fox Sports
news.body: ‘Bit of a floorshow’: Saints coach responds to ‘bizarre’ antics as AFL agitator causes stir  Fox Sports.
news.ai_summary: Saints coach Ross Lyon brushed off Bailey Smith's attention-grabbing antics as a "floorshow," with no fantasy-relevant fallout from the off-field stir. Smith owners can keep rolling him out without concern.

news.body: Essendon slump to bottom of AFL ladder, crisis worsens  News.com.au.
news.ai_summary: Essendon have plummeted to the bottom of the ladder, with the deepening crisis spelling danger for fantasy owners holding Bombers. Consider offloading underperforming Essendon assets before prices tumble further.

news.body: Deadly Dockers make it a perfect 10 after Saints scare  afl.com.au.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle survived a St Kilda scare to extend their winning streak to 10, keeping the Dockers' core relevant for finals-bound fantasy sides. Lock in reliable Freo premiums while their form and confidence are sky-high.

news.body: Tigers ramp up pressure on battered Bombers  afl.com.au.
news.ai_summary: Richmond piled on the pressure against an injury-hit Essendon, exposing the Bombers' depth issues. Fantasy coaches should be wary of Essendon premiums and monitor the late mail for emerging Tigers cash cows.

news.headline: Hawks star makes return as AFL comeback draws nearer - afl.com.au
news.body: Hawks star makes return as AFL comeback draws nearer  afl.com.au.
news.ai_summary: Hawthorn star returns to action with an AFL comeback edging closer—monitor his progress for a potential value-priced injury return.

news.headline: Hawks star makes return as AFL comeback draws nearer
news.body: Will Day makes his return from injury in the VFL on Friday night.
news.ai_summary: Will Day is unlikely to be an immediate selection option but his AFL return is edging closer after a managed VFL comeback for Box Hill (16 disposals, 6 tackles, 2 clearances) on Friday night. Coach Sam Mitchell confirmed Day will return when medically cleared, with no pressure on performance, suggesting at least another VFL hit-out before senior recall. With only six games in 2025 and a horror injury run (foot, shoulder), he's a risky POD but his midfield role and scoring ceiling make him one to monitor for trade-in once job security is confirmed.

news.headline: Final teams in for Tigers v Bombers, Dockers v Saints. Follow LIVE - afl.com.au
news.body: Final teams in for Tigers v Bombers, Dockers v Saints. Follow LIVE  afl.com.au.
news.ai_summary: Final teams are locked in for Richmond v Essendon and Fremantle v St Kilda — check late changes now before locking in your fantasy sides. Last-minute outs could reshape your captain and emergency calls.

news.headline: Crows star banned, gun fined for staging; Eagles’ hint on Pendles plan — AFL Daily - Fox Sports
news.body: Crows star banned, gun fined for staging; Eagles’ hint on Pendles plan AFL Daily  Fox Sports.
news.ai_summary: A Crows star has been suspended and another gun hit with a fine for staging, denting fantasy lineups this week. Meanwhile, the Eagles have dropped a hint on how they'll tackle Scott Pendlebury — worth noting for Pies owners.

news.body: AFL 2026, Adelaide Crows, Josh Rachele, dive  wwos.nine.com.au.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide forward Josh Rachele is in the spotlight over a diving incident, drawing scrutiny ahead of 2026. Monitor for any MRO sanctions that could impact early-season availability.
